ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Coca-Cola Zero Sugar; closest parent 'carbonated beverage' (FOODON_03301710) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN000A covers cola-type soft drinks as a category; no brand-specific code exists for Coca-Cola Zero Sugar.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 'Carbonated soft drink' is the closest parent; no brand-specific or sugar-free cola code exists.

allergens: phenylalanine (contains aspartame — source of phenylalanine, relevant for PKU)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of Coca-Cola Zero Sugar, as typically served in Singapore and convenience outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, cola, with aspartame, contains caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for carbonated cola beverage with aspartame and caffeine (diet/zero-sugar cola) is an exact category match for Coca-Cola Zero Sugar. Per-100g values used directly.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Diet cola and zero-sugar cola beverages contain no glycemic carbohydrates (sugar_g = 0, carbohydrate_g = 0.1 per 100g). The University of Sydney GI Database lists diet soft drinks as having a negligible GI due to absence of digestible carbohydrates. glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) per Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate content per serving is effectively zero.

hypertension.guidance: Coca-Cola Zero Sugar contains approximately 40 mg of sodium per 330 ml can, which is negligible relative to the daily sodium limit of 2000 mg recommended for hypertensive individuals; occasional consumption is unlikely to affect blood pressure, though habitual high-volume intake should be monitored.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Coca-Cola Zero Sugar contains no sugar and has a negligible glycemic load (GL ≈ 0 per serving), making it suitable as an occasional sugar-free alternative to regular cola for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, some evidence suggests that habitual consumption of artificially sweetened beverages may influence insulin sensitivity and gut microbiota, so moderation is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Absence of fat, sugar, and calories
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Coca-Cola Zero Sugar contributes negligible calories, no fat, and no sugar per serving, posing minimal direct cardiovascular risk; however, observational studies have associated regular consumption of artificially sweetened beverages with cardiovascular outcomes, and individuals with cardiovascular disease should prefer water or unsweetened beverages as their primary hydration source.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola Zero Sugar (also widely known as Coke Zero) is a globally distributed, commercially manufactured, ultra-processed carbonated soft drink produced by The Coca-Cola Company. It is formulated to replicate the taste profile of original Coca-Cola while containing no sugar and virtually no calories. The beverage is sweetened with a combination of aspartame and acesulfame potassium (Ace-K), which provide sweetness without contributing glycemic carbohydrates. The drink is carbonated, dark brown in colour, and delivers the characteristic cola flavour derived from caramel colour (E150d), phosphoric acid, natural flavours, and caffeine. Per 100 ml, it contains approximately 0.4 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, and 0.1 g carbohydrate, with a sodium content of approximately 12 mg. A standard 330 ml can contains roughly 1.3 kcal and 40 mg sodium. In Singapore, Coke Zero is commonly served as a beverage accompaniment at Chinese seafood restaurants, hot pot establishments, and BBQ dining venues, as reflected in its presence on field survey data menus. Clinically, it is considered a low-glycemic beverage suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or caloric restriction, though habitual consumption of artificially sweetened beverages warrants monitoring due to emerging evidence on metabolic and gut microbiome effects. It is gluten-free, dairy-free, vegan, and halal-certified in most markets. Individuals with phenylketonuria (PKU) must avoid this product due to its aspartame content, which is a source of phenylalanine.
common_user_queries: Is Coke Zero suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in Coke Zero?
What sweeteners are in Coke Zero?
Can I drink Coke Zero if I have high blood pressure?
What is the glycemic index of Coke Zero?
Is Coke Zero the same as Diet Coke?
零度可乐适合糖尿病吗

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851), which is an exact category match for diet/zero-sugar cola with aspartame and caffeine. GI is supported by University of Sydney GI Database convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. Product formulation details confirmed from Coca-Cola Company published ingredient declarations.
notes: Coca-Cola Zero Sugar is a zero-calorie, zero-sugar carbonated beverage. protein_g is confirmed zero — contains no protein by product formulation (USDA FDC ID 174851). fat_g is confirmed zero — contains no fat by product formulation (USDA FDC ID 174851). sugar_g is confirmed zero — sweetened exclusively with aspartame and acesulfame potassium, no added or natural sugars. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — no fibre-containing ingredients. energy_kcal is 0.4 per 100g (trace, from minimal carbohydrate residual). carbohydrate_g is 0.1 per 100g (trace residual, not from sugar). glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate content per 330 ml serving is effectively zero (0.33 g total carbohydrate). selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC source database for this product. Cuisine tagged as Chinese in the field survey source record due to the restaurant context (Chinese seafood and hot pot venues), but the product itself is a globally manufactured commercial beverage of US origin. Multilingual names provided per Singaporean cuisine rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in a Singaporean Chinese restaurant context. Contains aspartame: individuals with phenylketonuria (PKU) must avoid. Halal status: Coca-Cola Zero Sugar is halal-certified by Coca-Cola Singapore; kosher status varies by market and is not confirmed for Singapore.

improvement_actions_taken: Set food_name to canonical 'Coca-Cola Zero Sugar' rather than input alias 'Coke Zero 无糖可乐'.
Applied Singaporean multilingual naming rules (English, Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) due to Singapore restaurant context.
Set glycemic_index=1 per zero-calorie beverage sentinel rule; documented in notes and gi_source.
Documented all zero macro values with 'confirmed zero' / 'contains no protein' / 'contains no fat' justification phrases in notes.
Documented selenium_mcg=-1 sentinel value with source explanation in notes.
Used only allowed ontology match_type values: closest_parent for all three ontology systems.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list for perfect consistency before finalising.
